content.text:
GPU Computing
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEM3  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
This module enables students to understand GPU architecture and its use in AI, master the tools and techniques necessary for programming GPUs for AI, explore AI applications on GPUs, and understand how to optimise training and inference performance.
Course Content (21h)
Chapter 1 – Introduction to GPU Computing and AI
Role of GPUs in AI
Basic architecture (SM, warp, memory)
CPU vs GPU comparison
Chapter 2 – GPU Programming and Optimisation
CUDA / OpenCL programming model
Threads, blocks, and grids
Optimisation techniques (coalescing, asynchronous streams)
Profiling and debugging (nvprof, Nsight)
Chapter 3 – Distributed GPU Computing
Data and model parallelism
Inter-GPU communication (PCIe, NVLink, NCCL)
MPI + CUDA and GPU cluster orchestration
Chapter 4 – Multi-GPU AI Applications
Training CNNs and Transformers on multiple GPUs
Practical case: ResNet50 / BERT benchmark
Performance analysis and tuning
Chapter 5 – Deployment, Security, and Reliability
GPU containers (Docker, Kubernetes)
Cloud GPU (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Checkpointing and fault tolerance
Data security and GPU isolation
Chapter 6 – Advanced Technical Aspects
Video memory management (global, shared, register)
Atomic access and synchronisation
AI libraries (cuDNN, TensorRT)
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
